senses.gloss: Mursal (Hadith terminology): when a narrator between the Successor and Prophet Muhammad is omitted from the chain of the chain of transmitters, compromising the authenticity of the hadith.

examples.text: وَإِنِّي مُرْسِلَةٌ إِلَيْهِمْ بِهَدِيَّةٍ فَنَاظِرَةٌ بِمَ يَرْجِعُ الْمُرْسَلُونَ
examples.english: But I will certainly send him a gift, and see what ˹response˺ my envoys will return with.”

examples.text: وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيه ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِي وَلَا تَحْزَنِي ۖ إِنَّا رَادُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
examples.english: We inspired the mother of Moses: “Nurse him, but when you fear for him, put him then into the river, and do not fear or grieve. We will certainly return him to you, and make him one of the messengers.”

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of أَرْسَلَ (ʔarsala).

Derived from the passive participle of أَرْسَلَ (ʔarsala).
